spec_match.rationale: The page now uses the current CSS Color 5 alpha(from <color> / <alpha-value>) grammar for state colors and preserves color-mix() fallback declarations before every native alpha() declaration.

interactive.rationale: The brand-color buttons update the --brand custom property, button states, alerts, and token swatches live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page lede and feature-index blurb both describe deriving UI state colors from alpha(from var(--brand) / N%).

edge_cases.rationale: Chrome 148 reproduced the unsupported-browser branch: before the fix the claimed fallback rendered transparent backgrounds, and after the fix the fallback path uses color-mix() while preserving the Chrome 150 syntax.

references.rationale: The concept cites both the ChromeStatus entry and the CSS Color 5 alpha() section.

design_system.rationale: The touched button and alert styles keep explicit text colors, design-system surfaces, and fallback declarations that remain readable when alpha() is unsupported.

summary: Repaired after chrome-devtools-mcp showed Chrome 148 reporting no alpha() support while the page's ghost buttons, alert backgrounds, and token swatches computed to transparent despite claiming a fallback. The page now uses current alpha(from color / percentage) syntax, real color-mix() fallbacks, updated token swatches, and refreshed conformance assertions for the same grammar.
